{"key_event":"War of the Supremes and centralist consolidation","reasoning":"The War of the Supremes exposed Bogotá’s weak territorial control and empowered regional caudillos, but the government victory, 1843 Constitution, appointed governors, and census modestly rebuilt central authority while fiscal and geographic limits kept the state weak. Civil society strengthened through clerical-local resistance, regional armed mobilization, newspapers, merchants, artisans’ societies, and early party organization, though centralization curtailed some provincial autonomy.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":3.6}
